hypothesis: Incorporating DFT+U corrections specifically parameterized for the 3d transition metals (Cr, Mn, Fe, Co, Ni) identified as problematic into the low-fidelity baseline—replacing GFN1-xTB values with PBE+U values for MOFs containing these metals while retaining GFN1-xTB for all others—will yield a metal-type-conditional ∆-learning model that reduces the mean absolute error for the transition-metal subset by more than 50% relative to the uniform GFN1-xTB-based ∆-learning model, without significantly increasing computational cost for the remainder of the dataset.
